source.name: City of St. Augustine Municipal Marina

standard.rate_basis: per ft per day transient dockage (20 ft minimum)
standard.electric_rate_basis: 30A transient electric per day, per cord
standard.notes: Official city rates also show monthly transient dockage at $20/ft for up to 29 ft, $25/ft for 30-49 ft, $30/ft for 50-99 ft, $37.50/ft for 100-149 ft, and $42.50/ft for 150-200 ft. Transient electric is $7/day for 30A, $12/day for 50A, and $40/day for 100A. Hourly dockage is $5/hr up to 4 hours when space is available; dinghy dockage permits are $15/day.

cautions.text: Current in the downtown waterfront area can be meaningful, especially around the Bridge of Lions.

expert_notes.plain: This is the downtown convenience play, not the cheapest play. Strong overall stop if you want services, dinghy access, and immediate access to town.

source.name: City of St. Augustine / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Monthly transient mooring rate listed as $620.

cautions.text: Expect current and harbor movement in the downtown mooring area.

expert_notes.plain: Good downtown mooring choice if you want to be in the middle of St. Augustine without paying full-slip rates.

source.name: City of St. Augustine / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Monthly transient mooring rate listed as $400.

cautions.text: Salt Run has limited availability, minimum shoreside amenities, and no water tender service.
The water in Salt Run tends to be much shallower, so draft deserves attention.

expert_notes.plain: This is the quieter and more budget-friendly St. Augustine mooring play, but it is much less convenient ashore.

source.name: Camachee Cove Yacht Harbor official site

standard.notes: Official site confirms transient, seasonal, and long-term slips, but does not publish a public rate card online. Check Dockwa or call directly for current transient pricing.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is the protected, calmer St. Augustine marina option rather than the budget option.

standard.electric_rate_basis: base monthly utility fee before 30A/50A add-ons
standard.notes: Official slip-fee page shows $13/ft monthly for monohulls, with a 45 ft minimum on standard slips ($585 base/month) and a 30 ft minimum on the back dock ($390 base/month). Base utility/Wi-Fi fee is $25/month, with 30A at $60/month and 50A at $75/month. Fuel and pumpout are available nearby rather than directly at the marina.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: Good provisioning-oriented St. Augustine stop with a more practical feel than downtown. Official site pricing is monthly-oriented, and fuel/pumpout are nearby rather than directly on-site.

standard.notes: Official site confirms slips, moorings, fuel, and services but does not publish a public transient rate card online. Verify current transient and mooring pricing directly. Pumpout is reported at $5 and water is reported free in current marina-site review content.

cautions.text: Current transient and mooring pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is the most complete Stuart marina/anchorage package in the current core file, with both slip and mooring options plus strong services.

standard.notes: Official site confirms transient slips and amenities but does not publish a public transient rate card online. Verify current transient pricing directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A stronger Stuart marina option for protection and services than for budget.

source.name: City of Fort Pierce dockage rates

standard.rate_basis: per ft per day for vessels 25-67 ft
standard.notes: Official rates effective Sept. 1, 2025: 25-67 ft transient daily is $3.50/ft/day, 1st-2nd week $2.00/ft/day, 3rd-16th week $1.25/ft/day, and 5-11 months $23.75/ft/month. Vessels 68 ft+ or catamarans 19 ft beam+ are $3.90/ft/day and $2.35/ft weekly. Transient electric is $6/night for 30A and $20/night for 50A.

cautions.text: Official dockage rates are published by the city but should still be confirmed before arrival.

expert_notes.plain: A strong municipal-marina style stop with real services, published rates, and better budget transparency than many private marinas.

source.name: City of Vero Beach Marina reservations and rates

standard.rate_basis: per ft per day transient slip
standard.notes: Official Vero rates show Main Marina transient slips at $2.50/ft/day, $2.00/ft/day weekly (7-night minimum), and $18.00/ft/month; South Complex monthly transient is $22.00/ft/month. Vessels over 100 ft are $4.50/ft/day. Moorings are $23.36/day; extended-stay monthly mooring is $370.56 plus a $50 facilities fee. Transient electric is $7/day for 30A, $12/day for 50A, and $40/day for 100A.

cautions.text: Vero’s official published rate structure uses monthly-equivalent formatting in places; confirm the exact transient slip quote directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is a strong managed-city-marina option with both mooring and transient capability, and better published rate transparency than many private marinas.

source.name: Safe Harbor New Port Cove official site

standard.rate_basis: official site does not publish transient rates online
standard.notes: Official site confirms amenities and access but does not publish a public transient rate card online. Verify current transient pricing directly with the marina.

cautions.text: This is not a good choice for deeper-draft boats; reported approach and dockside depth are only 5 feet.

expert_notes.plain: Useful small-slip Riviera Beach option if your draft fits it. Better for modest-draft boats than for heavier cruising sailboats.

geo.segment: Palm Beach Shores / Lake Worth Inlet

standard.notes: Official dockage page confirms daily and yearly dockage with taxes additional, but a public transient rate card was not extractable from the site text at this review. Verify current transient pricing directly with the marina.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A legitimate Lake Worth inlet-area transient stop with easy boater contact information and on-site fuel, but not the cheapest play.

source.name: Riviera Beach Marina Village official rates page

standard.rate_basis: per ft per day transient slip
standard.electric_rate_basis: 30A transient utility charge per day, per cord
standard.notes: Official short-term rates show transient slip dockage at $4.00/ft/day and transient face-dock dockage at $5.00/ft/day. Transient utilities are 30A $20/day, 50A $30/day, and 100A $50/day; 480V 3-phase is $200/day. Utilities for transients include water, sewer, and electric. There is a 50 ft minimum on all face-dock slips.

cautions.text: Current can be an issue, especially on the outer docks, so docking plans should account for tide and current.
Reported dockside depth is only 7 feet, so deeper-draft boats should confirm current conditions carefully.
Official rates state a 50 ft minimum on all face-dock slips.

expert_notes.plain: A real Riviera Beach transient option with decent services and fuel, but current on the outer docks is the operational issue to take seriously.

standard.rate_basis: official site does not publish transient rates online
standard.notes: Official site confirms transient slips, vessel service, and berths for yachts up to 100 meters, but does not publish a public transient rate card online. Verify current pricing directly.

cautions.text: This is a premium deep-water marina and service yard rather than a budget stop.

expert_notes.plain: A deep-water, high-capability Palm Beach area stop that makes sense when draft, boat size, or service yard access matter more than budget.

source.name: Town of Palm Beach Marina official dockage page

standard.rate_basis: daily transient dockage is published by slip size; official inventory starts at 60 ft
standard.electric_rate_basis: high-capacity shore power available; confirm exact connection pricing and assignment directly
standard.notes: Official dockage page publishes daily and monthly rates by slip size rather than by foot. Winter daily totals currently begin at $396 for a 60 ft slip, $429 for 65 ft, $608 for 80 ft, $646 for 85 ft, and extend up to $2,380 for 200 ft. Summer daily totals begin at $180 for a 60 ft slip and $195 for 65 ft. Official marina materials state the facility welcomes vessels from 60 to 294/295 ft, making it a large-yacht oriented choice rather than an average 25-50 ft cruiser default.

cautions.text: This is a premier Palm Beach marina, so pricing should be expected to reflect that.
Official marina materials state the facility welcomes vessels from 60 feet upward, so it is not a natural fit for the average 25-50 foot cruising boat.
This is a high-end superyacht-oriented marina, so availability, pricing, and vessel fit should be evaluated with that in mind.

expert_notes.plain: This is Palm Beach's polished superyacht marina, not the average-cruiser default. It makes sense when prestige, walkability, and larger-yacht capability matter more than cost or small-boat fit.

standard.rate_basis: official site does not publish a public transient rate card online
standard.electric_rate_basis: 30A, 50A, 100A, and 200A available; confirm current pricing directly
standard.notes: Official site states rates vary by vessel length, dockage type, and season, but does not publish a public transient rate card online. The marina advertises slips for yachts from 50 to 300 feet, so smaller typical cruising boats should confirm fit and availability directly.

cautions.text: Current transient rates should be confirmed directly.
Official site advertises slips for yachts from 50 to 300 feet, so this is not the most natural fit for smaller cruising boats.
Palm Harbor is a premium downtown marina rather than a budget stop.

expert_notes.plain: Palm Harbor is a useful West Palm transient and fuel option, but it is also a premium yacht-oriented marina with slips marketed from 50 feet upward.

source.name: Fernandina Harbor Marina / City of Fernandina Beach

standard.rate_basis: published by vessel-length tiers; call marina for current quote and availability
standard.notes: Transient dockage is published by length tier. Under 70 ft: $2.75-$3.25/ft/day, $16.10-$17.50/ft/week, $34.50/ft/month. 70-99 ft: $3.00-$3.50/ft/day, $17.15-$18.20/ft/week, $38.40/ft/month. 100-149 ft: $3.50-$4.00/ft/day, $19.25-$20.00/ft/week, $42.00/ft/month. 150-199 ft: $3.75-$4.25/ft/day, $21.00-$22.50/ft/week, $43.50/ft/month. 200-250 ft: $4.00-$4.50/ft/day, $22.75-$24.15/ft/week, $43.50/ft/month. Special events and holidays may be higher. Transient stays limited to 6 months. Long-term dockage: non-commercial $25/ft/month; commercial $27.50/ft/month. Dock N Dine per 3 hours: up to 25 ft $16.05; 25-35 ft $32.10; over 36 ft $42.80. Electric: daily 30A/50A/100A = $6/$12/$25; weekly $30/$72/$125; monthly $60/$125/$350.

cautions.text: This is a busy downtown waterfront marina, so movement and activity can be higher than at more secluded stops.
Special events and holidays can carry higher rates than the standard published schedule.

expert_notes.plain: A top Fernandina stop if downtown access matters. Strong contactability, fuel, pumpout, and immediate walkability make it the practical in-town choice. The marina also has a first-come, first-served mooring field with amenity access.

source.name: Fernandina Harbor Marina official rates page

standard.notes: First come, first served mooring field. Weekly mooring rate is $192.60 tax included. Monthly mooring rate is $636.64 tax included.

special_offers.label: Dinghy dock under 12 ft
special_offers.notes: Tax included; vessels less than 12 feet only

special_offers.label: Dinghy dock under 12 ft
special_offers.notes: Tax included; vessels less than 12 feet only

cautions.text: The mooring field is first come, first served.
Dinghy dock pricing applies only to vessels less than 12 feet.

expert_notes.plain: The Fernandina mooring field is first come, first served. Amenity access includes the dinghy dock, showers, laundry, free Wi-Fi, and the boater's lounge.

source.name: Windward at Amelia Island Marina Dockwa listing

standard.notes: Dockwa booking page confirms transient dockage availability, booking windows, and check-in/check-out timing, but public pricing is quote-based rather than posted as a fixed online rate. Verify current transient pricing directly or through Dockwa.

cautions.text: This is not a good blind-arrival stop for deeper boats. Reported approach depth is shallow enough that current local conditions matter.

expert_notes.plain: A quieter Amelia-area stop than downtown Fernandina, but the approach depth is the real filter. Better for shallower boats that want protection more than town access.

source.name: Tiger Point Marina / Waterway Guide / Official Marina Site

standard.notes: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly. This remains a shallow-access, tide-sensitive stop where conditions matter more than bargain-shopping.

cautions.text: Transient space can be limited, so this is best treated as a call-ahead stop.

expert_notes.plain: A practical Amelia River stop when you want marina services and a quieter setting than downtown Fernandina.
